justification: All pass criteria met: (1) Authentication per consumer type correctly designed — JWT/OAuth2 for mobile, API key for partners, network identity/mTLS for internal. (2) Internal traffic separation fully addressed with dedicated internal ingress channel bound to private network interface, explicitly separated from external gateway. (3) Business logic explicitly excluded — 'Contains zero business logic' in gateway component boundaries and repeated in pitfalls_to_avoid. (4) SPOF risk identified as critical with stateless cluster, multi-AZ, and auto-scaling mitigations. (5) Webhook delivery for partners thoroughly addressed with dedicated Webhook Dispatcher component, exponential backoff, dead letter queue, HMAC signatures, and replay capability. (6) Four flexibility_points and three rigidity_points present with clear evolution paths and rationale. (7) Technology recommendation covers Kong, AWS API Gateway, Envoy with build/buy consideration and acknowledgment that choice depends on team expertise and infrastructure.

observations.text: Excellent three-channel ingress design with logical separation — goes beyond minimum by addressing resource contention and blast radius trade-offs of shared vs separate gateways
Strangler fig migration strategy is well-designed with shadow mode phase, which most candidates miss
Rate limit store failure mode handled with circuit breaker and local fallback — shows production-grade thinking
Webhook design is async event-driven with proper decoupling from core services, retry policy, and partner-facing delivery dashboard
Pitfalls_to_avoid section is unusually strong — covers body transformation, east-west traffic misuse, plaintext API keys, and skipping shadow mode
Header enrichment contract as the stable interface between gateway and services is a clean architectural decision with appropriate rigidity designation
Could have been more explicit about authorization vs authentication split — mentions services should still authorize but the boundary could be crisper
Low confidence areas are honest but the gateway technology recommendation stays vague — a stronger opinion with caveats would be more actionable

agent_output_excerpt: {"executive_summary":"The logistics platform needs a unified API gateway layer to replace inconsistent per-service auth and rate limiting. The design introduces a single logical gateway with three distinct ingress channels (mobile, partner, internal), centralizing cross-cutting concerns while maintaining strict traffic isolation. This eliminates the current anti-pattern of distributed auth implementations and creates a single enforcement point for security, rate limiting, and observability.","ar
